name: Salsa Alla Bolognese
recipeIngredient: 1 small white or yellow onion, chopped fine
1/4 lb lean, good quality beef, preferably chuck, ground twice
1/4 lb lean, good quality pork, preferably belly, ground twice
1/4 lb lean, good quality veal, ground twice
1/2 cup good quality, dry white wine
2 1/2 c (1 28-ounce can) Italian plum tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped, purée
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, wide Dutch oven or large, deep skillet, sauté the butter, chopped onion, celery, and carrot until they are soft but not browned.
2. Add the ground meats and prosciutto, turning the flame very low. Heat gently enough to color the meat on the outside without searing it.
3. Break up and stir the meat as you heat it until it reaches a uniform pink color, not brown.
4. Add salt, pepper, and wine. Simmer gently for several minutes until the alcohol evaporates and the liquid is absorbed by the meat and vegetables.
5. Stir in milk and nutmeg. Add the milk before adding the tomatoes so it can be directly absorbed by the meat.
6. Cook gently for 10 minutes. Add the tomatoes.
7. When the sauce begins to simmer, turn the flame down as low as possible. If your burner cannot be regulated low enough, insert a "flame tamer."
8. Simmer partially covered for about 4 hours, stirring gently now and then. Check for seasonings and add an additional pinch of nutmeg if necessary.
9. If using on fresh noodles (not lasagne) and you wish to be extravagant, add the cream and finely sliced truffle when finished. Stir and pour over cooked fresh pasta.
Yield: Sauce for 1 pound of pasta
